babyDevelopment: The baby's ears have reached their final position and can now sense sounds from the outside world. This is a great time to start talking or singing to your baby. The fetus is incredibly active, flexing arms and legs.
bodyChanges: You might start feeling more pronounced movements now. Your appetite may increase significantly. Some women experience 'pregnancy-induced carpal tunnel' syndrome due to fluid retention.
tips: Ensure you're getting enough iron to support baby's blood supply
Talk and sing to your baby—they're starting to listen!
Continue with gentle stretching to manage back pain

do: Use a supportive pillow for side-sleeping
dont: Ignore signs of persistent leg swelling

milestoneDescription: Baby's ears are fully formed, and they can start to hear your heart and voice.
